checks.a_analysis: The HTML output fails to maintain the visual organization of the source document by omitting nearly all maps, diagrams, and charts that characterize the GIS presentation. While the text is presented in a logical vertical flow, the layout for slides 8, 12, 14, 15, 17, 19, 22, and 23 is fundamentally altered; what were visually-driven slides with integrated graphics have been reduced to bare headings or text lists, losing the intended visual flow and context of the information.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 14 and 15 show maps arranged to compare 'Before' and 'After' states. Page 19 shows a trend line chart as the primary content.
checks.b_evidence_output: The sections 'Case Study – Charlotte/Meck Bus Stop Consolidation' and 'Bus Routes Before/After Stop Consolidation' consist only of headings with no visual content. The chart on page 19 is entirely absent, leaving only text headers.

checks.a_analysis: Tables present in the HTML output, such as the one on page 16 ('Improvement from Stop Consolidation') and the tally table from page 17, are rendered correctly with clear rows and columns. Screenshots of forms (e.g., page 9) have been converted to readable HTML tables, which preserves the data accurately.

A significant number of meaningful images are missing or broken in the HTML output. The 'TIMS System' diagram on page 8 is rendered only as an empty green box. Critical maps on pages 12, 14, 15, 17, 22, and 23 are completely missing. The statistical chart on page 19 is also missing. These graphics are essential content for a document titled 'The Value of GIS'.
checks.b_evidence_source: Diagram on page 8; maps on pages 12, 14, 15, 17, 22, 23; and chart on page 19.
checks.b_evidence_output: The TIMS diagram is a solid green rectangle. No corresponding images exist for the maps and charts in the HTML screenshot.

checks.a_analysis: The content in the HTML output follows the sequence of the PDF slides. The repetition of the title slide at the end of the document (page 24) is a faithful reproduction of the source material. No unintended duplication of body paragraphs or tables was observed.
